title: podman info needs to be run within the user namespace
body:
Accidently removed podman info from user namespace

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5746

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Gating tests did. I have no idea how/why/if CI passed.
Works fine if I run it locally, but this is definitely a change in the PR.

One possibility: this might be a cgroupsv2-only issue. I can't reproduce the failure on f30. I just spun up a rawhide virt and am going to build and test there. IIRC CI tests only run rootless on some-old-fedora?
I am running it in F32 on cgroupsV2.

I can reproduce the failure on rawhide but only if I build with `systemd` build tag.

The patch in this PR resolves the issue, and all other system tests pass. LGTM. Thanks @rhatdan for fixing this so quickly.

f31 local is failing in [two containers with same IP](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5799261904306176/html/integration_test.log.html#t--Podman-create-two-containers-with-the-same-IP--2). I thought I had filed a flake issue about that but can't find it. @mheon, do you remember our long discussion about this last month?
Vaguely? I think we concluded this was probably CNI, not Podman?

flake-summarize results, to remind me to look into this later:
